greetings.meaning: A friendly word to say when you see someone
greetings.example: Hello, my name is Emma. What is your name?
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'hello' when you want to be friendly to someone.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Hello! Friend: Hello! Do you want to play?

greetings.meaning: A greeting used in the morning before noon
greetings.example: Good morning, Mom! Did you sleep well? I had a dream about a puppy.
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'good morning' when you wake up or see someone before lunch.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Good morning, teacher! Teacher: Good morning! Ready to learn?

greetings.meaning: A greeting used from 12 PM until evening
greetings.example: Good afternoon, class! Today we will learn about butterflies.
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'good afternoon' after 12 o'clock until dinner time.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Good afternoon, Grandma! Grandma: Good afternoon, sweetie!

greetings.meaning: A greeting used in the evening after sunset
greetings.example: Good evening, Dad! What's for dinner? I am very hungry today.
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'good evening' when the sun starts to go down.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Good evening! Neighbor: Good evening! Beautiful night, isn't it?

greetings.meaning: Said when going to bed or leaving at night
greetings.example: Good night, Mom. I love you. See you in the morning!
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'good night' before you go to sleep.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Good night! Mom: Good night! Sweet dreams!

greetings.meaning: A casual and friendly way to greet someone
greetings.example: Hi! Do you want to play with me? I have a new soccer ball.
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: 'Hi' is a quick and happy way to say hello to a friend.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Hi! Friend: Hi! Let's go to the park.

greetings.meaning: An informal greeting often used to get attention
greetings.example: Hey! Wait for me! I want to come to the playground too!
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'hey' when you want a friend to look at you or wait for you.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Hey, look at this! Friend: Wow, that's cool!

greetings.meaning: A polite question about someone's health or feelings
greetings.example: Hi Lily! How are you today? I hope you feel better after being sick.
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Ask 'how are you' to show you care about how your friend feels.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: How are you? Friend: I'm great, thanks!

greetings.meaning: A polite response meaning you feel good
greetings.example: How are you? I'm fine, thank you. And you? How was your weekend?
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say this when someone asks how you feel and you feel good and happy.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: I'm fine, thank you! Teacher: That's wonderful to hear.

greetings.meaning: An enthusiastic response meaning you feel very good
greetings.example: How are you today? I'm great! I got a new puppy yesterday!
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'I'm great' when you feel super happy and excited.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: I'm great! Friend: Tell me why!

greetings.meaning: A response meaning you don't feel well
greetings.example: How are you feeling? Not so good. I have a headache today.
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'not so good' when you feel sad or sick.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Not so good. Mom: Oh no! Let me help you.

greetings.meaning: Said when meeting someone for the first time
greetings.example: Hello, I'm Tom. Nice to meet you! Do you want to be friends?
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'nice to meet you' when you meet a new person for the first time.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Nice to meet you! New friend: Nice to meet you too!

greetings.meaning: A friendly way to say you will see someone again soon
greetings.example: I have to go home now. See you later, Alex! Same time tomorrow?
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'see you later' when you will see your friend again soon.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: See you later! Friend: See you tomorrow!

greetings.meaning: Said when you will see someone the next day
greetings.example: Goodbye, class! See you tomorrow! Don't forget your homework.
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say this when you know you will see someone the next day.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: See you tomorrow! Friend: Bye! Have a good night.

greetings.meaning: A standard word used when leaving someone
greetings.example: Goodbye, Grandma! I love you! I will visit again next weekend.
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'goodbye' when you are leaving a place or person for a while.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Goodbye! Grandma: Goodbye, my sweet child!

greetings.meaning: A casual, child-friendly way to say goodbye
greetings.example: Bye bye, puppy! Be a good dog while I am at school today!
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: 'Bye bye' is a fun and easy way to say goodbye to friends and family.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Bye bye! Baby: Bye bye! Wave your hand!

greetings.meaning: A kind goodbye wishing someone safety and health
greetings.example: Have fun at the park with your friends! Take care and be careful!
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'take care' when you want your friend to be safe and healthy.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Take care! Friend: You too! See you later.

greetings.meaning: A greeting for someone who has arrived at your place
greetings.example: Welcome to my birthday party! I have cake, games, and presents for everyone!
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'welcome' when someone comes to your house, party, or school.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Welcome to my home! Guest: Thank you for inviting me.

greetings.meaning: A casual way to ask what someone is doing
greetings.example: Hey Jake! What's up? Do you want to build a fort with me?
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say 'what's up' to ask a friend what they are doing or how they feel.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: What's up? Friend: Nothing much. Want to play?

greetings.meaning: Said when you haven't seen someone in a while
greetings.example: Wow, Sarah! Long time no see! You grew so tall since last summer!
greetings.kid_friendly_explanation: Say this when you haven't seen a friend for many days or weeks.
greetings.dialog_example: Child: Long time no see! Cousin: I know! I missed you!
